title: Excludes are ignored if wildcard is used into .dockerignore on podman 2.1.1
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If you use  `.dockerignore` with wildcard `**`, your excludes will be ignored. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. 
create folder with files:
```
sh# mkdir folder1
sh# touch folder1/file1 folder1/file2
```
Dockerfile:
```
FROM nginx:1.19.2
ADD folder1 /tmp
EXPOSE 80
STOPSIGNAL SIGTERM
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```
.dockerignore:
```
**
!folder1/file1
```
2. run build
```
sh# docker build --no-cache -t xxx .
STEP 1: FROM nginx:1.19.2
STEP 2: ADD folder1 /tmp
Error: error building at STEP "ADD folder1 /tmp": error adding sources [/var/mybuild/folder1]: error checking on source /var/mybuild/folder1 under "/var/mybuild": copier: stat: "/folder1": no such file or directory
```
**Describe the results you received:**
Excludes doesn't work.

**Describe the results you expected:**
File `folder1/file1` must be added into layer.  
On podman-2.0.6 - works properly

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15
Built:        Mon Sep 28 02:07:17 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
